summary: Matched five stage-related functions at 100%, split out from PR #2345: grKinokoRoute_8020836C, fn_801F9038, grHomeRun_8021EC58, grPushOn_802190D0, and grOldKongo_80210650. The work primarily advanced gr/stage decompilation, with supporting type/header cleanup, callback typedef consolidation, include/order formatting, and struct field adjustments across fighter, game-mode, baselib, and toy files. Review focused on avoiding ugly or unnecessary local extern/__FILE__ hacks, clarifying locally duplicated macros/defs, and naming unused struct bytes as padding.
what_changed: Implemented five previously stubbed or unmatched stage functions to 100% match status: grKinokoRoute_8020836C, fn_801F9038, grHomeRun_8021EC58, grPushOn_802190D0, and grOldKongo_80210650.

grKinokoRoute_8020836C was described in the PR body as toggling special collision state on a Mushroom Kingdom route stage joint, adding a fixed set of joint IDs to the collision system, and applying grMaterial_801C8E08 to live item GObjs of kind 0xA0.
fn_801F9038 was described as Ice Mountain random next-segment selection using HSD_Randi(6), cooldown slots, and recent segment exclusion fields xC4/xC6.

grHomeRun_8021EC58 was described as constructing the Home-Run Contest score HSD_Text overlay, including scale-dependent text box/font setup, white color, left alignment, and formatting the current distance with "%d".
grPushOn_802190D0 was described as building and wiring nine Mute City point-light descriptors from a static light-config table.

grOldKongo_80210650 was described as a weighted random direction picker over eight counter fields x2C through x3A, returning angle constants in multiples of pi/4 or asserting if no bucket is selected.
Introduced a GmRouteCallback typedef in src/melee/gm/forward.h and reused it in gm_801674C4, fn_80169434, and struct lbl_8046B488_t instead of spelling out bool (*)(s32).
Adjusted struct and header declarations, including Fighter_804D64FC comments/spacing and Fighter_x1A88_t queue field formatting.
Made broad formatting and include-order cleanup in nearby fighter, game-mode, stage, and baselib files, likely to satisfy style or matching constraints.

Dropped unrelated or insufficiently matched work from the parent PR: lbAudioAx_800243F4/fn_800244F4 and grOldPupupu_8021119C were already upstream; grKinokoRoute_80207B5C was only 99.64% and reverted to a stub.
smart_moves: Split a larger work-in-progress PR into a stage-only slice containing only 100% matches, making review and merge scope safer.
Explicitly listed matched functions and statuses in the PR body, giving reviewers a precise checklist.
Separated out non-ready work, especially grKinokoRoute_80207B5C with real struct-field offset diffs, rather than forcing a 99.64% function into a 100% progress PR.
Used a named callback typedef, GmRouteCallback, to reduce repeated raw function-pointer signatures and keep gm_1601 declarations consistent across source, public header, and static header.
For grOldKongo_80210650, preserved exact matching behavior with explicit weighted bucket subtraction and HSD_ASSERT behavior, even though review noted it might be expressible as a loop.
For grPushOn_802190D0, encoded repeated light setup data as a table of light configuration values, matching the function while making the point-light wiring easier to read.
decomp_lessons: For past-PR reuse, avoid bundling 99% matches with 100% matches when there are real struct offset differences; leave the near-match stubbed until the underlying type issue is understood.
When a match requires local extern float constants or a __FILE__ override for HSD_ASSERT, reviewers may accept it for matching but will push back if the hack looks unused or avoidable.
If a decompiled chain of repeated weighted-random checks matches but looks like a for-loop candidate, it is acceptable to keep the matched version, but note the possible loop form for future cleanup.

Local macro definitions that duplicate baselib functionality, such as an HSD_LObjGetType-style macro, should be justified if used for inline or matching reasons and should not masquerade as the real library function.
Unused struct byte ranges should be named as padding, e.g. x1C_pad..., not as ordinary unknown fields, when review evidence indicates they are unused.
Callback typedefs are useful when a function-pointer type appears in both struct fields and function prototypes; define the typedef in a forward/common header and apply it consistently.
Large matching PRs often include style churn from formatter constraints; keep such changes mechanical and tied to nearby matching/type cleanup where possible.
naming_conventions: Stage function names keep the grStagePrefix_address style, e.g. grKinokoRoute_8020836C, grHomeRun_8021EC58, grPushOn_802190D0, grOldKongo_80210650.
Unknown internal functions may remain fn_address when no better semantic name is evidenced, e.g. fn_801F9038.
Game-mode function-pointer typedef introduced as GmRouteCallback, replacing bool (*)(s32) in gm_801674C4, fn_80169434, and lbl_8046B488_t::x1B8.
Reviewer preference: unused unknown struct byte arrays should be named with a pad suffix such as x1C_pad rather than x1C.
Existing unknown struct fields remained offset-based where semantics were not proven, e.g. grOk_804D6A90->x2C, x2E, x30, x32, x34, x36, x38, x3A.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit nested subtraction checks in grOldKongo_80210650 to match weighted-random bucket selection rather than forcing a cleaner loop form.
Used HSD_Randi(total) only when total is nonzero in grOldKongo_80210650, otherwise falling back to r = 0 before bucket subtraction.
Used named sdata2 float constants for grOldKongo angle return values, including grOk_804DB9CC and grOk_804DBA04 through grOk_804DBA1C.
Used a __FILE__ redefinition to a rodata/sdata symbol for HSD_ASSERT matching in groldkongo.c; review questioned whether this should remain if not needed.
For grPushOn_802190D0, introduced static HSD_LightDesc/light configuration data and iterated through nine entries to populate lighting, with a local HSD_LObjGetType macro noted by review.
Used static or table-driven data for repeated light descriptor properties such as color, position, reference brightness, reference distance, and distance function.
For route/callback work, consolidated function pointer type spelling via GmRouteCallback to keep ABI-equivalent declarations synchronized without changing behavior.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: The block of extern float declarations and __FILE__ redefinition looked bad; reviewer asked whether it was used and suggested removing it if not.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer noted the nested subtraction chain might be representable as a for loop, but said keeping it was fine if the current form matched and loop conversion was not worth pursuing.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer questioned why a definition was created when a function exists in lobj.c and asked for clarification if the local form was needed for inline reasons, especially because it used the same name while not being exactly the same.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer suggested renaming unused struct byte fields to x1C_pad... as good practice, though not strictly required.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up groldkongo.c extern float constants or remove the __FILE__ override.
Search for adoption of x1C_pad-style padding names in stage structs after PR #2404.
Check whether HSD_LObjGetType was later centralized or renamed to avoid a local macro collision in grpushon.c.
Look for the eventual match or type fix for grKinokoRoute_80207B5C after it was reverted to a stub here.
Search for additional uses of GmRouteCallback to see if the typedef became the standard callback convention.
